key_insight: The paper gives computable two-sided a posteriori error certificates for PINNs solving ODEs, pairing new lower bounds with sharper localized upper bounds under verifiable monotonicity conditions.
engineering_relevance: This enables more trustworthy PINN deployment in scientific computing by quantifying when a learned ODE solution is actually close to the true state without needing the exact solution.
method: Derive lower error bounds from localized strong monotonicity on certified state-space domains.
Derive localized upper bounds using a one-sided Lipschitz condition and the PINN residual.
For linear systems, express certificates via eigenvalues of the symmetric part and use signed-residual finite probes when initial conditions are enforced exactly.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports closed-form and computable certificates for ODEs, including explicit linear-system formulas, but does not mention a numerical benchmark or dataset in the text provided.
watch_item: The lower certificate can become uninformative under hard enforcement of initial conditions, and the assumptions must be checked on a suitable certified domain.
paper_title: Reliable Error Estimation for PINNs: Lower and Upper A Posteriori Bounds
